title: Compare Quadrilaterals
essential_question: How can you compare two quadrilaterals by looking at what is the same and what is different?

pretest.question: What is the SAME about a square and a rectangle?
choices: B) Both have all sides equal

B.note: Squares yes, rectangles in general no

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: What is true for BOTH squares and rectangles?

feedback.correct: Yes! Both squares and rectangles have 4 right angles.
feedback.incorrect: Both shapes have 4 right angles. That is what they share.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! Both squares and rectangles have 4 right angles.

pretest.question: What is DIFFERENT between a rectangle and a rhombus?
choices: B) Whether all sides are equal
C) Whether the figure is closed
D) Whether the sides are straight

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Rhombus has all equal sides; non-square rectangle does not.

hints: Which attribute is true for one but not the other?

feedback.correct: Yes! A rhombus has 4 equal sides, but a non-square rectangle has 2 long and 2 short sides.
feedback.incorrect: The difference is whether all 4 sides are equal. Rhombus = yes; non-square rectangle = no.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! A rhombus has 4 equal sides, but a non-square rectangle has 2 long and 2 short sides.

pretest.question: How many sides do BOTH a square and a trapezoid have?

8.note: Don't add — they each have 4 sides

hints: Both shapes are quadrilaterals.

feedback.correct: Right! Both have 4 sides — they are both quadrilaterals.
feedback.incorrect: Both shapes are quadrilaterals — each has 4 sides.

pretest.feedback_correct: Right! Both have 4 sides — they are both quadrilaterals.

pretest.question: What is DIFFERENT between a parallelogram and a trapezoid?
choices: A) The parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides; the trapezoid has only 1
C) The trapezoid has curved sides
D) Nothing — they are the same

D.note: They differ in the number of pairs of parallel sides

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Parallelogram: 2 pairs parallel; trapezoid: 1 pair parallel.

hints: Compare how many pairs of parallel sides each has.

feedback.correct: Yes! Parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides; trapezoid has only 1.
feedback.incorrect: The key difference: parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides, while trapezoid has only 1.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! Parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides; trapezoid has only 1.

pretest.question: Which is true about BOTH a rhombus and a parallelogram?
choices: B) Both have 2 pairs of parallel sides

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Both have 2 pairs of parallel sides.

hints: What attribute do both shapes always have?

feedback.correct: Yes! Both rhombuses and parallelograms have 2 pairs of parallel sides.
feedback.incorrect: Both have 2 pairs of parallel sides — that is the shared attribute.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! Both rhombuses and parallelograms have 2 pairs of parallel sides.

learn.title: What Does It Mean to Compare?
learn.content: To COMPARE two shapes is to look at what is the SAME and what is DIFFERENT. Same = shared attributes. Different = an attribute one has but the other doesn't. Example: A square has 4 right angles AND a rectangle has 4 right angles → SAME. A square has all 4 sides equal, but a (non-square) rectangle does not → DIFFERENT.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Same: 4 Sides
learn.content: All quadrilaterals have 4 sides. So when you compare any two quadrilaterals, '4 sides' is always SAME. Squares, rectangles, rhombuses, parallelograms, and trapezoids all have 4 sides. The number of sides does not help you tell them apart.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Go Math 12.4: same number of sides.

learn.title: Compare Square and Rectangle
learn.content: SAME: 4 sides, 4 right angles, 2 pairs of parallel sides. DIFFERENT: A square has all 4 sides equal; a non-square rectangle has 2 long and 2 short sides. Both are special quadrilaterals — every square is a rectangle, but not every rectangle is a square.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Compare Square and Rhombus
learn.content: SAME: 4 sides, all 4 sides equal, 2 pairs of parallel sides. DIFFERENT: A square has 4 right angles; a (non-square) rhombus does not. Every square is a rhombus, but not every rhombus is a square.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Compare Rectangle and Rhombus
learn.content: SAME: 4 sides, 2 pairs of parallel sides. DIFFERENT: A rectangle has 4 right angles, but a (non-square) rhombus does not. A rhombus has 4 equal sides, but a (non-square) rectangle does not. The two shapes have different special attributes.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Compare Parallelogram and Trapezoid
learn.content: SAME: 4 sides, both have at least 1 pair of parallel sides. DIFFERENT: A parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides; a trapezoid (in 3rd grade) has only 1 pair. So a parallelogram has more parallel sides than a trapezoid.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Use a Compare Chart
learn.content: A simple chart helps. Make rows for each attribute (sides, right angles, equal sides, parallel pairs). Make a column for each shape. Mark each cell yes/no. Cells with the same answer = SAME. Cells with different answers = DIFFERENT.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Lesson Summary
learn.content: Today you learned to compare quadrilaterals by attributes: (1) Look at SAME and DIFFERENT separately. (2) All quadrilaterals share '4 sides'. (3) Squares and rectangles share '4 right angles'. (4) Squares and rhombuses share '4 equal sides'. (5) Trapezoids have FEWER parallel pairs than parallelograms.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Compare a trapezoid and a parallelogram. Which statement is TRUE?
choices: A) Both always have 4 right angles
B) Both have at least 1 pair of parallel sides
D) Trapezoids have more sides than parallelograms
solution_steps: Step 1: Trapezoid has at least 1 pair of parallel sides — TRUE.
Step 2: Parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides — TRUE.
Step 3: Both have AT LEAST 1 pair of parallel sides — TRUE.

feedback.correct: Right! Both have at least 1 pair of parallel sides.
feedback.incorrect: Both have at least 1 pair of parallel sides. Trapezoid has 1; parallelogram has 2.

C) No — angles are always the same
D) Yes, but only for squares
solution_steps: Step 1: All parallelograms have 4 sides — same.
Step 2: Some parallelograms have right angles (rectangles), some don't.
Step 3: Ben is correct: sides are always 4, but angles can differ.

C.note: Angles are not always the same

D.note: Ben's claim is true for all parallelograms

feedback.correct: Right! All parallelograms have 4 sides, but angles can differ (some have right angles, some don't).
feedback.incorrect: Ben is correct. All parallelograms have 4 sides, but only some have right angles.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What does Ben claim? Read 2: Is the side count always the same in parallelograms? Read 3: Are the angles always the same?
